ftester.extension.description: VS Code extension for displaying and running ftester (a Swift-based mobile UI testing tool) scenarios as a tree
ftester.config.binaryPath.description: Path to the ftester CLI binary. Relative paths are resolved from the workspace root. If the path does not exist (e.g. a Tier 2 package with ftester installed globally), ftester is looked up on PATH.
ftester.config.project.description: Name of the target test project (the <name> in Projects/<name>). If empty, it is auto-detected (used if there is exactly one project directly under Projects/; if there are multiple, you will be prompted to choose).
ftester.config.profile.description: Name of the run profile to use (the <name> in Projects/<project>/profiles/runs/<name>.json). If empty, no profile is specified. When non-empty, this is used instead of platform/port/serial for run and debug run (runs with device supply and automatic install included; the target device must already be started beforehand, e.g. via ftester devices up). The device monitor's watch targets are also limited to the devices referenced by this profile (all devices if empty). The device monitor's "Start All Devices" and "Stop All" also target only this profile's devices (all devices in the machine profile if empty). Switching this setting automatically stops any running devices not included in the new profile (devices that are included stay running as-is; no automatic start).
ftester.config.platform.description: Target platform (used only when ftester.profile is empty).
ftester.config.port.description: Bridge connection port. If 0, no port is specified (the default is used; used only when ftester.profile is empty).
ftester.config.serial.description: Android device serial number. If empty, no serial is specified (used only when ftester.profile is empty).
ftester.config.buildBeforeRun.descriptio: Whether to run a Swift build before invoking the CLI. If false, --skip-build is passed to the CLI invocation.
ftester.config.heal.description: Enables FM-based locator self-healing (--heal) during execution. After execution completes, if there are repair candidates a confirmation panel opens, and approving it applies the changes to the source.
ftester.config.monitorInterval.descripti: Update interval (seconds) for the device monitor (ftester: Show Device Monitor). Values below 0.5 are treated as 0.5.
ftester.config.monitorMaxWidth.descripti: Long-edge pixel size of the device monitor's frame images. Larger values are sharper but increase transfer volume (240-1600).
ftester.config.liveFps.description: Upper limit (fps) for the automatic frame update rate in the Live Control tab. Larger values are smoother but increase host load (3-30).
ftester.config.iosStreamEnabled.descript: Updates the screen during iOS simulator live control via video streaming (ftester-simstream). Falls back to polling if disabled or the helper is not built.
ftester.config.androidStreamEnabled.desc: Updates the screen during Android device/emulator live control via video streaming (ftester-androidstream). Falls back to polling if disabled, the helper is not built, or adb is not detected.
ftester.config.streamCodec.description: Codec for device screen streaming. h264 (default) uses hardware decoding via WebCodecs. Switch to mjpeg (legacy method) in environments where issues occur.
ftester.config.liveControlOnRun.descript: Automatically shows the Live Control panel on the right side of the editor when Run Test starts.
ftester.config.autoRepairBridge.descript: When bridge unresponsiveness is detected (a device that was connected stays stuck in booted and does not recover), attempts automatic repair via device-up, but only while no lane is currently running.
ftester.config.autoRepairDeviceHealth.de: When guest OS abnormalities on an Android emulator are detected (Wi-Fi disabled, clock drift), attempts automatic repair by re-enabling Wi-Fi and then rebooting.
ftester.config.showOnlyFailedTests.descr: Filters the Test Explorer tree to show only failed tests (not-run and passed tests are hidden). Can also be toggled via the filter button in the Test Explorer title bar.
ftester.config.language.description: UI display language for the extension. "auto" follows the VS Code display language (Japanese for ja locales, otherwise English).
ftester.view.ftesterSteps.name: ftester Steps
ftester.debugger.project.description: Name of the target test project (the <name> in Projects/<name>). If omitted, it is filled in from the ftester.project setting or auto-detection.
ftester.debugger.scenario.description: Scenario ID to debug (ClassName.methodName). Only one can be specified.
ftester.debugger.dryRun.description: Pass all commands through record-only, without touching the device (dry-run).
ftester.debugger.stopOnEntry.description: Start paused just before the first step.
ftester.debugger.skipBuild.description: Skip the swift build before running.
ftester.debugger.heal.description: Enable FM-based locator self-healing (--heal) (not applied when dryRun is specified).
ftester.debugger.profile.description: Name of the run profile to use (the <name> in Projects/<project>/profiles/runs/<name>.json; uses the ftester.profile setting if omitted). When specified, platform/port/serial are ignored.
ftester.debugger.platform.description: Target platform (uses the ftester.platform setting if omitted; ignored when profile is specified).
ftester.debugger.port.description: Bridge connection port (uses the ftester.port setting if omitted).
ftester.debugger.serial.description: Android device serial number (uses the ftester.serial setting if omitted).
ftester.debugger.initialConfig.name: ftester: Debug Scenario
